Extremely disappointed with Tira's abysmal customer service and deceitful practices. Despite being a returning customer, my experience has been frustrating. They have a terrible policy of refusing to cancel orders, even when wrong items are received. Recently, an item was completely missing from my order. I’ve repeatedly requested a refund or even Tira points, but they outright denied it, falsely claiming no product was missing. To make matters worse, the attractive freebies they advertise during checkout are never what you actually receive; they just send random trash instead. If you want a reliable skincare and beauty shopping experience, please stick to Nykaa. Tira is a total waste of time and money, and I will definitely not be shopping here again.

Tira has been one of the most frustrating shopping experiences I’ve ever had. The service is consistently unreliable, and the quality control is honestly shocking. I’ve received wrong items more than once, and in some cases, the products arrived damaged or in terrible condition literally dusty and looking like they’d been sitting on a shelf for ages.

What’s even worse is their handling of gift cards. I purchased a gift card, and within minutes of trying to use it, the app showed the correct balance but refused to apply it. It kept throwing errors saying the card had already been used or that the limit had been reached neither of which was true. I even tried using amounts equal to and below the balance, but nothing worked.

Customer service has been no help at all. Instead of resolving issues, it feels like you’re stuck going in circles with no real solution. Overall, the experience feels careless and incredibly disappointing.

I would not recommend Tira to anyone looking for a smooth or trustworthy shopping experience.

I had a very frustrating experience with this app. I purchased a gift card worth ₹2080 to place an order during a limited-time offer. While placing the order, the app showed “order placed successfully” not once but twice, along with order IDs.

However, there was no confirmation via email or SMS, and the order does not appear in my order history at all. My money has already been deducted via the gift card, and now it is essentially gone.

I have followed up multiple times with customer support, but there has been no proper resolution. All they keep saying is that it is a “cache issue,” without actually fixing the problem or restoring my money/order.

The offer period has now ended, and I lost the chance to purchase the products despite placing the order on time.

This feels extremely unreliable and untrustworthy. If your system cannot handle payments and orders properly, users should not be put at risk of losing their money.

Would not recommend at all.

Writing this review based on 4 perfumes ordered from TIRA. I am a perfume collector and have 1000+ designer / niche fragrances. This was my first (and last) order from them.

Another one (Narciso Rodriguez Bleu Noir Parfum) does smell but performs only for 10 mins.

Third one - Armaf Tres Nuit smells too weak and doesn’t last for even 5 minutes.

Last one - Mont Blanc Presence EDT is the only one that performs like original.

So, two days ago I got an offer on a BOJ facemask and a makeup brush. It was quite unexpected because I had just added them to my cart, and suddenly the price dropped, giving me almost ₹500 off. I placed the order immediately out of desperation, thinking it could go out of stock.

Two days later, while I was at the office, I got a call from a delivery person who claimed he was delivering my Tira parcel. He had the correct order number, my address, and even the exact COD amount I was supposed to pay. Since I was busy, I didn’t think much of it, after all, how could it be a scam if he had all this personal information? Right? 

I paid via a QR code he sent me, which looked completely legit and showed up as a Tira business account. But when I got home, to my shock, there was no parcel. That’s when I realized I had been cleverly scammed.

The scam was extremely organised and honestly, without someone on the inside, it feels nearly impossible to pull off.

Tira was completely unhinged when I contacted them. They refused to refund even a single penny because, according to them, they never received any payment. So my ₹2.2k just went down the drain.

It’s absolutely insane that we trust companies with our personal information so blindly, only for them to sell it off for some extra income.

When I checked Reddit and other forums, they were filled with similar complaints, people being scammed left and right, receiving fake products, and getting no refunds.

PLEASE, PLEASE SAVE YOUR MONEY, GUYS! Don’t fall for their marketing gimmicks with celebrities. They are pathetic scums who will get money out of you through scams.

After I complained, I received multiple calls from Tira representatives asking about the issue—but nothing ever came of it. Eventually, they claimed my refund was cancelled because the product was “delivered.”

In reality, their delivery partner clearly told me they would not deliver to my address and asked me to collect the package from a nearby town (Dasuya), which is about an hour’s drive away. I refused—because what is the point of online shopping if I have to drive to another city to get my order?

I specifically asked Tira to review the call recording with their delivery agent, where I explained I couldn’t make the trip. Despite that, I’ve received no resolution—just silence.

Bottom line: The Tira app, which I found through Instagram, has proven to be unreliable and misleading. This experience may not have cost me a lot of money, but it cost me trust, and that’s why I’m warning other customers to stay cautious.

If I could, I would take legal action for this level of mismanagement and dishonesty.

3. **Poor Customer Service**: The customer service is unresponsive and unhelpful. It's very difficult to get any support if you have an issue with your purchase.

4. **Misleading Advertising**: The images and descriptions of the products are misleading. What you receive is not what you see in the app.

5. **No Return or Refund Policy**: There is no clear return or refund policy, making it almost impossible to get your money back if you are not satisfied

1. Their banner will show higher discount value, but once you’ll add the product in the cart, the discount value will be lesser. 

2. I’ve shopped from TIRA, but haven’t received any reward points yet. 

3. Freebies that are attached to every product of a brand, doesn’t get added in the cart. For ex, XYZ brand is giving a freebie with all its product no matter the value you spend on the brand, the freebie will NOT GET ADDED. However if there is a minimum amount, then the freebie gets added. Or if the freebie has if else code, it will get added. 

4. Though the app design is good, there needs to be more lineup on the variety of brands. I feel a lot of brands are being missed.
